posts.text: The murder of UnitedHealth Group CEO Brian Thompson invites a closer look at the health insurance industry - and the practice of denying claims to keep down costs.  What does industry practice teach us about privatizing services that meet basic human needs?   I get into that here.  Share your thoughts in comments.  #health #healthcare #insurance #government #care

posts.text: Trump’s incoming “Border Czar” Tom Homan had a warning for any elected official – or seemingly any individual – who chooses to protect an undocumented person from ICE agents. He says American citizens will be arrested if they offer sanctuary or safety to those targeted, regardless of the individual’s history. While Homan says that they will prioritize arrests and detention of undocumented people who have a criminal record, he’s also made clear it’s not limited to those with criminal records. Thi

posts.text: Our favorite childless cat lady is using the return of The Eras Tour in the United States to get out the vote. As Taylor Swift begins the final leg of her record-breaking year with several shows in Miami this weekend, the Democratic National Committee is using the shows as a push to get out the vote Kamala Harris. There will be billboards outside the Hard Rock Stadium where Swift is performing encouraging people to vote and providing links to learn how and where to cast a ballot.  All weekend, S

posts.text: Things we couldnt have imagined 4 years - or wven 3 minths - ago. At rhe @Texas Tribune festival Liz Cheney said her dad, Dick Cheney, will vote Harris/Walz. The Cheneys have been outspoken in sharing their view that Trump is a theat to democracy.

posts.text: INFLUENCERS VS JOURNALISTS Today presidential nominee VP Kamala Harris and Gov Tim Walz sat for their first interview with traditional press since Biden dropped out. This interview came together after mounting pressure from traditional media, which claimed that the Harris/Walz campaign is granting reporters less access to the candidates than is standard for a major party nominee during an election year. posts.text: In Alabama, Democrat Marilyn Lands just won a special election for the state House by a whopping 25 points. She flipped a Republican seat by running a campaign focused on the recent Alabama Supreme Court ruling that threatened to end access to IVF and driving a message about reproductive rights.After her win she wrote on X, “Alabama women have spoken!”  Lands, who presented herself as a mom, a counselor and a Christian, talked about her own experience getting an abortion when a pregnancy was dee
